title: Issues, Reviews, Pull Requests, and Team Workflow · History Brief: Issue Acceptance Link
summary: Move a change from issue to review to integration with traceable decisions, bounded branch scope, and respectful collaboration. Planned artifact: Add and verify the git issue acceptance link increment in a team contribution and review protocol. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Connect issue context, acceptance criteria, branch scope, commits, tests, and review evidence without making the tracker the source code.
Choose trunk-based, feature-branch, release-branch, or fork collaboration from delivery, risk, ownership, and release constraints.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-basics cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Predict Issue Acceptance Link
steps.instruction:
theory case — Issue 3 requires one behavior and accessibility regression. A pull request contains five unrelated cleanup commits; define review range, split scope, required evidence, and authorized merge policy.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Issue Acceptance Link contract for the supplied data.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for connect issue context, acceptance criteria, branch scope, commits, tests, and review evidence without making the tracker the source code.

content.text: Connect issue context, acceptance criteria, branch scope, commits, tests, and review evidence without making the tracker the source code. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Locate repository and operation state, compare working tree, index, refs, and graph, preserve a recovery reference, preview the smallest change, then verify both tree behavior and history topology. Apply that move specifically to Issue Acceptance Link while producing a team contribution and review protocol.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Issue 3 requires one behavior and accessibility regression. A pull request contains five unrelated cleanup commits; define review range, split scope, required evidence, and authorized merge policy. Competency probe: Connect issue context, acceptance criteria, branch scope, commits, tests, and review evidence without making the tracker the source code. A defensible solution applies Issue Acceptance Link, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a team contribution and review protocol.

content.code:
# Worked focus: git-issue-acceptance-link — Connect issue context, acceptance criteria, branch scope, commits, tests, and review evidence without making the tracker the source code.
# Evidence: changed Git case 4
# hypothesis: the mismatch is isolated to one repository layer
git status --short --branch
git diff
git diff --staged
git log --graph --decorate --oneline -n 6
# observation: name worktree, index, ref, and graph evidence separately
content.caption: Runnable repository-state trace example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

steps.title: Defend the Issue Acceptance Link model
steps.instruction: Write the theory evidence note for Issue Acceptance Link: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Issue 4 requires one behavior and accessibility regression. content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Connect issue context, acceptance criteria, branch scope, commits, tests, and review evidence without making the tracker the source code.
